bead.title: Story 16.3c: Restore efficacyRows p95 under 300ms perf target
bead.description:
PROBLEM
The full-repo `cd cli && go test ./...` gate (parent AC 4) fails because the efficacy-rows query has regressed past its perf target: p95 measured at 431ms against a 300ms ceiling.

ROOT CAUSE
- cli/internal/server/graphql/efficacy_sessions_test.go:160 `TestEfficacyRowsDateFilterAndPerfTargets` exercises the date-filtered efficacyRows resolver and asserts a p95 perf target; current p95 = 431ms against the 300ms ceiling, so the efficacyRows aggregation/date-filter path in internal/server/graphql has regressed.

PROPOSED FIX
- Profile the efficacyRows resolver and its date-filter aggregation; restore p95 under 300ms (e.g. push date filtering down to the scan, avoid recomputing aggregates per row, reuse warmed indexes).
- Do not relax the perf assertion; the target is the contract.

NON-SCOPE
- Frontend e2e, spec-doc, and audit-event work (sibling/parent beads).
- The server GetBeadSnapshot and internal/agent timeout regressions (sibling beads 16.3b / 16.3d).
- New auth or role-based access control.
bead.acceptance:
1. cli/internal/server/graphql/efficacy_sessions_test.go:160 `TestEfficacyRowsDateFilterAndPerfTargets` passes with efficacyRows p95 <= 300ms.
2. `cd cli && go test ./internal/server/graphql/...` is green.
3. `lefthook run pre-commit` passes.
